title: Cite coverage algorithm — events.jsonl replay + relevance_paths denominator + metrics aggregation
description: Replace runDoctorCiteCoverage stub with the real algorithm. Single readEventLedger pass filters events by ts >= marker_ts (TASK-04) AND ts >= since_ts (TASK-05 flag). Aggregate per-turn assistant_turn_observed events into per-cite outcomes. Join against agents.meta.json relevance_paths to compute denominator: for each KB entry with non-empty relevance_paths, the denominator = count of edit_intent_checked events (within window) whose path matches any relevance_path glob; for broad entries (empty relevance_paths), denominator = total edit_intent_checked count. Compute 5 metrics: edits_touched, qualifying_cites, recalled_unverified, expected_but_missed, total_turns. Per-client split when --client=all. Dismissed-reason histogram.

files.change: Replace stub with real implementation. Steps: (1) Call ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker → bail to status:'skipped' if marker_ts==0. (2) Compute effectiveSince = max(marker_ts, options.since). (3) Call readEventLedger(projectRoot, {since: effectiveSince}) ONCE — collect all events. (4) Switch on event_type in single loop, partitioning into: assistantTurns[] (event_type='assistant_turn_observed'), editEvents[] (event_type='edit_intent_checked' OR other edit-bearing types). (5) Filter assistantTurns by client when options.client !== 'all'. (6) Load agents.meta.json via readExistingMeta or buildKnowledgeMeta best-effort; build kbIndex Map<stable_id, {relevance_paths:string[], relevance_scope:'narrow'|'broad'}>. (7) For each kb_id seen in assistantTurns.cite_ids, compute denominator: if kbIndex[kb_id].relevance_paths.length > 0 → count edits whose path matches any relevance_path glob (use minimatch — already imported elsewhere in doctor.ts); else → count all edits in window. (8) For each assistantTurn: increment total_turns; categorize by cite_tags: 'planned'/'recalled'/'chained-from' → qualifying_cites++; 'recalled' WITHOUT a paired fab_get_knowledge_sections call in same session within ±N seconds → recalled_unverified++ (verification check: scan knowledge_sections_fetched events for matching session_id + ts proximity, see clarification below). 'dismissed:<reason>' → push reason into dismissed_reason_histogram (split on ':' to extract reason; 'other:<text>' bucketed as 'other'). 'none' → not counted toward qualifying. (9) Compute expected_but_missed: edit events whose path matches a kb_id's relevance_paths BUT no assistant_turn cited that kb_id in the same turn (turn defined via session_id + envelope ts proximity). (10) Compute edits_touched: |editEvents|. (11) Build per_client breakdown by grouping assistantTurns by client field. (12) Return CiteCoverageReport with all metrics populated.

files.target: helper functions (consider extracting to packages/server/src/services/cite-coverage.ts if implementation exceeds ~200 lines)
files.change: Add helpers: `matchesRelevancePath(editPath, relevancePaths)` using minimatch; `categorizeCiteTag(tag)` returns {category: 'planned'|'recalled'|'chained-from'|'dismissed'|'none', reason?: string}; `groupByTurnId(events)` for turn boundary detection; `computeRecalledVerified(turn, allEvents)` checks for fab_get_knowledge_sections in same session within window.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L2885-L2950 (buildLastActiveIndex) for the structural twin replay pattern.
Read packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts:32-56 + knowledge-meta-builder.ts:933-1034 to confirm relevance_paths/relevance_scope read path.
Decide: inline in doctor.ts OR extract to packages/server/src/services/cite-coverage.ts — prefer extraction if function body grows >150 lines (use SLOC count after first draft).
Implement matchesRelevancePath helper using minimatch (already a doctor.ts dep) — accepts edit absolute or relative path + array of glob patterns; returns boolean.
Implement categorizeCiteTag(tag): tag string like 'planned' / 'recalled' / 'chained-from KP-001' / 'dismissed:scope-mismatch' / 'dismissed:other:custom-text' / 'none' → tagged result object.
Implement single-pass replay: one readEventLedger call, one for-loop over events, branch on event_type, accumulate into local Maps/arrays.
Compute denominator per cite_id: narrow → glob-match count; broad → total edit count.
Compute recalled_unverified: for each turn with 'recalled' tag, scan knowledge_sections_fetched events with matching session_id within ±60s window — count UNVERIFIED.

Compute expected_but_missed: for each edit event whose path matches some kb.relevance_paths, check if any assistant_turn in same session (proximity ts) cited that kb_id; if no → expected_but_missed++.
Aggregate per_client: group assistantTurns by client field, recompute metrics per group.
Build dismissed_reason_histogram by iterating dismissed tags.
Return CiteCoverageReport with all numeric metrics and aggregations.
Add inline unit-testable helpers (each pure function) — TASK-08 will test them.

reference.examples: buildLastActiveIndex is the structural twin: one readEventLedger pass + switch on event_type + accumulator Map.

rationale.chosen_approach: Single-pass replay with all event_type branches in one loop avoids double-reading events.jsonl (which is a full-file in-memory parse). Denominator joins against agents.meta.json snapshot (not live frontmatter) for consistency with the rest of doctor.
alternatives_considered: Multi-pass readEventLedger calls per event_type (rejected: doubles I/O)
Live frontmatter scan instead of agents.meta.json (rejected: inconsistent with knowledge-sync semantics, adds new fs walk)
Defer recalled_unverified to TASK-07 (rejected: it's a core metric, not a presentation concern)
decision_factors: Performance: single I/O pass per spec constraint #1 in coverage-algo exploration
Atomicity: meta.json snapshot is the source of truth
rationale.tradeoffs: Recalled_verified detection requires session_id correlation across event types — adds modest complexity. Window size (±60s) is a heuristic; calibrate via TASK-08 tests.

manual_checks: Seed events.jsonl with mixed turns + edits + knowledge_fetches; run runDoctorCiteCoverage; manually verify metric arithmetic
Edge case: empty events post-marker → all metrics zero
Edge case: broad-only KB entries (current real-world state) → all denominators = total edit count
Edge case: narrow KB with paths that match no edits → denominator = 0, expected_but_missed = 0
success_metrics: Algorithm runs in <100ms for typical ledgers (<10k events)
All 5 metrics + per_client + dismissed_reason_histogram populated correctly
Recalled_unverified correctly correlates session_id within window

criteria: Single readEventLedger pass (verifiable via test spy)
All 5 metrics populated: edits_touched, qualifying_cites, recalled_unverified, expected_but_missed, total_turns
per_client breakdown present when client filter is 'all'
dismissed_reason_histogram contains all 4 enum values (scope-mismatch/outdated/not-applicable/other) when seeded with each
Narrow denominators correctly use minimatch glob against edit paths
Broad denominators correctly use total edit count
expected_but_missed correctly identifies edits whose path matches a kb.relevance_paths but no cite was emitted
recalled_unverified correctly correlates session_id within ±60s window
Status='skipped' returned when marker_ts==0 (no policy activated)
Commit msg: `feat(rc20): cite coverage algorithm — replay + denominator + metrics (TASK-06)`
